question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/config/EvmConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: cacheManagerRecordFileLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the 500ms expireAfterWrite TTL on the CACHE_MANAGER_RECORD_FILE_LATEST cache (maximumSize=1) by flooding eth_getBlockByNumber('latest') calls just before a new block arrives, causing the single-slot cache to serve a stale block for up to 500ms per cycle, effectively making the EVM perceive the chain as frozen and delaying transaction finality by 500% or more of the average block time?
